architecture: TWO banks: LP bank (3x A pump, parallel) lifts to a ~1,400 psig common header feeding Disposal + Kuparuk injection headers AND the HP bank suction; HP bank (3x B pump, parallel) boosts that ~1,400 psig to ~3,500 psig Power Fluid header. Banks are in SERIES (LP->HP); pumps within a bank are in PARALLEL.
specific_gravity_note: Schlumberger DESIGN SG = 1.05; FIELD produced water ~1.02-1.04 (operator). CSV uses 1.03. Head stored in feet (SG-INDEPENDENT) -- this is the real pump curve; boost_psi = head_ft * SG / 2.31. For the optimizer/system curves, ingest head_ft and apply your own SG.

field_head_factor.basis: live SCADA 2026-06-16 at SG 1.03; field dP / as-new-curve dP, all 6 pumps, drawing on-curve power
field_head_factor.interpretation: installed pumps make ~9% less head than as-new at on-curve power = efficiency loss; operator handles heavy SOLIDS, so erosive stage wear is the likely cause. The polynomials are AS-NEW; multiply head_ft by this factor for current field performance.
field_head_factor.verify_before_concluding: produced-water SG, discharge/suction transmitter calibration, and a witnessed single-pump performance test

system_curve_guidance: To build system curves: (1) use head_ft(Q) per pump at 60 Hz as the AS-NEW pump curve; (2) affinity-scale to actual Hz; (3) apply field_head_factor (~0.91) for the worn/current curve; (4) convert head_ft to psi with field SG (1.02-1.04); (5) within a bank the 3 pumps are PARALLEL (same dP, sum flow); the LP bank discharge is the HP bank suction (SERIES). Static head + friction define the system curve; intersection with the (derated) pump curve sets the operating point.
common_motor: TOSHIBA 1500 HP, 4160 V, 2-pole (3569 RPM @ 60 Hz), 5811/12 frame, WPII, VFD/ASD driven

motor_current_limits_A.note: from Moosepad HPS set-up sheets; trip = 120% of running amps

A_LP_4220.role: low-pressure bank (3 pumps parallel) -> ~1,400 psig header
A_LP_4220.model: REDA N1400N-A, 1000/1000 series (10 in housing), mixed-flow
A_LP_4220.seal: 48V w/ segmented bushing, max 850 psig, Plan 11

B_HP_4230.role: high-pressure bank (3 pumps parallel), suction = LP header ~1,400 psig -> ~3,500 psig Power Fluid
B_HP_4230.seal: 8610 w/ segmented bushing, max 2900 psig, Plan 11

design_point_60hz_SG1.05.note: shut-in 4470.7 > 4400 psig rating; set discharge trip to not exceed ~4000 psig

compute.affinity_to_other_speed: Q = Q60*(Hz/60); head = head60*(Hz/60)^2; BHP and amps ~ (Hz/60)^3
compute.motor_amps_est: amps_per_hp_est * bhp(Q60)  (then scale by (Hz/60)^3 for reduced speed); compare to trip 195.8 A

scada_snapshot_2026-06-16_1009.note: Full per-pump operator screens (speed, flow, suction, discharge, amps). Discharge controlled by PIC-4221 (LP, SP 1400) and PIC-4231 (HP, SP 3500).

P-4220A.alarm: PUMP HIHI DIFF PRESSURE CURVE S/D

scada_snapshot_2026-06-16_1009.series_staging_confirmed: LP discharge header 1398-1400 psig = HP suction 1391-1392 psig.
scada_snapshot_2026-06-16_1009.amps_vs_model: Motor amps match the as-new catalog BHP within ~1-7 A (amps ~= 0.13 * catalog BHP). Plenty of amp headroom: 92-125 A vs 178 A FLA / 195.8 A trip.
scada_snapshot_2026-06-16_1009.head_vs_curve_FINDING: At the displayed flow & speed, every pump makes ~10% LESS differential than its as-new curve (field/curve dP ratio mean 0.895, range 0.86-0.94) WHILE drawing on-curve power. Lower head at on-curve power = reduced efficiency, consistent with stage WEAR. Not an SG effect (SG scales head and power together) nor a flow-meter offset (that moves head and power oppositely). Verify produced-water SG and discharge/suction transmitter calibration, and consider a witnessed performance check, before concluding wear.
scada_snapshot_2026-06-16_1009.operating_context: Both banks run far LEFT of their 60 Hz BEP (LP ~18-21k vs 49,791; HP ~16-18k vs 27,708) at 53-58 Hz. LP P-4220A is at its high-diff-pressure curve shutdown limit; LP B/C show discharge-flow LOLO.

validated: Curve fits reproduce Schlumberger's own data-sheet/set-up-sheet points (shut-in, BEP, design power) to <0.5%. Live SCADA (6/16/2026, all 6 pumps): amps match catalog BHP within ~1-7 A; LP->HP series staging confirmed (LP discharge ~1,400 = HP suction ~1,392). NOTE: field differential runs ~10% below the as-new curves at displayed flow/speed (see head_vs_curve_FINDING) -- as-new curve is the reference; deviation flags possible wear / instrument cal.
